namespace delete_inactive_users {

int delete_inactive_users() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_connection("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->begin_transaction();

    const auto cutoff_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 1, 1);
    const auto query = "DELETE FROM User WHERE last_login < ?";
    conn->execute(query, cutoff_date);

    conn->commit();
    return 0;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return -1;
  }
}

} // namespace delete_inactive_users
</answer>

  std::string SName;
  std::optional<int> Sex = 1;

  std::optional<int> Major;

  int Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;

};

  std::string SNo;
  int CID;
  std::optional<float> Score;
};

  const auto create_student = R"(
    CREATE TABLE Student (
      SNo VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
      SName VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
      Sex INT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      Birthday DATETIME NULL,
      Major INT NULL,
      Scorses FLOAT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      Remark TEXT NULL
    );
  )";

  const auto create_course = R"(
    CREATE TABLE Course (
      CID INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      CName VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
      Term INT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      CTime INT NULL,
      Credit FLOAT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
    );
  )";

  const auto create_score = R"(
    CREATE TABLE Score (
      SNo VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
      CID INT NOT NULL,
      Score FLOAT NULL,
      PRIMARY KEY (SNo, CID),
      FOREIGN KEY (SNo) REFERENCES Student(SNo),
      FOREIGN KEY (CID) REFERENCES Course(CID)
    );
  )";

  conn->execute(create_student);
  conn->execute(create_course);
  conn->execute(create_score);
}

}  // namespace test_design_eas_database

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_active_users_posts.hpp"

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create User table.
  const auto user_table = sqlgen::Table<User>();
  conn->execute(user_table.create_table());

  // Create Post table.
  const auto post_table = sqlgen::Table<Post>();
  conn->execute(post_table.create_table());

  // Query for active users.
  const auto query = conn->select_from<User>()
      .join<Post, User::user_id == Post::user_id>()
      .where(Post::created_at >= "2025-12-01"_dt && Post::created_at <= "2025-12-31"_dt)
      .select<User::user_id, User::username>()
      .distinct()
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          ActiveUser active_user;
          active_user.user_id = res[0].as<int>();
          active_user.username = res[1].as<std::string>();
          result.emplace_back(std::move(active_user));
        }
        return result;
      });

  return query.value_or(std::vector<ActiveUser>());
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts
```</answer>

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> course_id;
  std::string title;
};

struct Enrollment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
  std::optional<float> grade;
};

struct CourseStats {
  std::string title;
  int student_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_grade;
};

struct CourseStatsView {
  // View to store course enrollment statistics
};

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConn("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = conn.session();

    // Create Course table
    session.create_table<Course>();

    // Create Enrollment table
    session.create_table<Enrollment>();

    // Build the query
    const auto c = session.make_alias<Course>("c");
    const auto e = session.make_alias<Enrollment>("e");

    auto query = session.select_from(c, e)
        .on(c.course_id == e.course_id.course_id)
        .fields(c.title, sqlgen::count(e.enrollment_id).as("student_count"), sqlgen::avg(e.grade).as("avg_grade"))
        .where_group_by(c.course_id, c.title)
        .group_by(c.course_id, c.title);

    // Execute the query
    auto result = session.execute(query);

    // Process the results
    std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
    if (result) {
      for (const auto& row : *result) {
        CourseStats cs;
        cs.title = row[0].get<std::string>();
        cs.student_count = row[1].get<int>();
        if (row[2]) {
          cs.avg_grade = row[2].get<double>();
        } else {
          cs.avg_grade = std::nullopt;
        }
        stats.push_back(std::move(cs));
      }
    }

    return stats;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (e.g., log error)
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::ConnectionPool::make("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = conn->acquire_session();

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT e1.name AS employee, e2.name AS manager
    FROM Employee e1
    LEFT JOIN Employee e2 ON e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id
  )";

  const auto result = session->execute(query);

  std::vector<EmployeeManager> output;
  for (const auto& row : result) {
    EmployeeManager em;
    em.employee = row["employee"].as<std::string>();
    em.manager = row["manager"].has_value() ? row["manager"].as<std::string>() : std::nullopt;
    output.push_back(std::move(em));
  }

  return output;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy

#endif
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_employee_hierarchy.hpp"

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::ConnectionPool::make("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = conn->acquire_session();

  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Employee, Employee>(
          sqlgen::fields<"e1", "name"_field>,
          sqlgen::fields<"e2", "name"_field>)(
          .alias1 = "e1",
          .alias2 = "e2",
          .on = "e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id",
          .how = sqlgen::JoinType::left);

  const auto result = session->template read<std::vector<EmployeeManager>>(query);

  return result.value_or(std::vector<EmployeeManager>());
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy
```</answer>

import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SelectFrom, Alias, Condition, desc
from sqlgen.sqlite import SQLiteConnector

def query_high_value_orders():
    connector = SQLiteConnector("test_db.sqlite")
    order = Alias("Order", "o")
    customer = Alias("Customer", "c")

        order.c.order_id,
        customer.c.name,
        order.c.total_amount,

import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SQLiteConnector

def query_product_sales_report():
    # Connect to the SQLite database
    conn = SQLiteConnector("test_db.sqlite")
    
    # Define the SQL query
    query = """
        SELECT p.category, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_qty, SUM(s.amount) AS total_amt
        FROM Product p
        INNER JOIN Sale s ON p.product_id = s.product_id
        GROUP BY p.category
        HAVING SUM(s.amount) > 5000;
    """
    
    # Execute the query and fetch results
    results = conn.execute(query).fetchall()
    
    # Convert results to a list of dictionaries
    reports = []
    for row in results:
        reports.append({
            'category': row[0],
            'total_qty': row[1],
            'total_amt': row[2]
        })
    
    return reports

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "query_search_books_by_criteria.hpp"

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& _filters) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>();

  std::vector<sqlgen::Condition> conditions;

  if (_filters.title_keyword) {
    auto condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("title").like("%" + *_filters.title_keyword + "%"));
    conditions.push_back(condition);
  }
  if (_filters.min_year) {
    auto condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("year") >= *_filters.min_year);
    conditions.push_back(condition);
  }
  if (_filters.genre) {
    auto condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("genre") == *_filters.genre);
    conditions.push_back(condition);
  }
  if (_filters.max_price) {
    auto condition = sqlgen::make_condition<Book>(
        rfl::field("price") <= *_filters.max_price);
    conditions.push_back(condition);
  }

  if (!conditions.empty()) {
    auto combined = std::accumulate(
        std::next(conditions.begin()), conditions.end(), conditions[0],
        [](const auto& _a, const auto& _b) { return _a && _b; }));
    query.where(combined);
  }

  return conn->execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& _cursor) {
        return sqlgen::to_vector<Book>(std::move(_cursor));
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<Book>());
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```</answer>

#include <rfl.hpp>

  double price;
  int stock;
};

  std::string table_name;
  int record_id;
  std::string action;
  sqlgen::DateTime timestamp;
};

      CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS audit_product_update
      AFTER UPDATE ON Product
      BEGIN
          INSERT INTO AuditLog (table_name, record_id, action, timestamp)
          VALUES ('Product', OLD.product_id, 'UPDATE', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
      END;
    )";
    conn->execute(trigger_sql);
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string sku;
  std::string name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

bool manage_indexes() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Create Product table if not exists
    const std::string create_table_sql =
        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Product ("
        "product_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, "
        "sku VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, "
        "name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, "
        "category VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, "
        "price REAL NOT NULL);";
    conn.execute(create_table_sql);

    // Create idx_product_category if not exists
    const std::string check_idx_category_sql =
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master "
        "WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_product_category' "
        "AND tbl_name='Product';";
    auto res = conn.execute(check_idx_category_sql);
    if (res.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0) {
      const std::string create_idx_category_sql =
          "CREATE INDEX idx_product_category ON Product(category);";
      conn.execute(create_idx_category_sql);
    }

    // Create unique idx_product_sku if not exists
    const std::string check_idx_sku_sql =
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master "
        "WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_product_sku' "
        "AND tbl_name='Product';";
    res = conn.execute(check_idx_sku_sql);
    if (res.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0) {
      const std::string create_idx_sku_sql =
          "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_product_sku ON Product(sku);";
      conn.execute(create_idx_sku_sql);
    }

    // Create idx_product_name_price if not exists
    const std::string check_idx_name_price_sql =
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sqlite_master "
        "WHERE type='index' AND name='idx_product_name_price' "
        "AND tbl_name='Product';";
    res = conn.execute(check_idx_name_price_sql);
    if (res.begin()->at(0).as<int>() == 0) {
      const std::string create_idx_name_price_sql =
          "CREATE INDEX idx_product_name_price ON Product(name, price);";
      conn.execute(create_idx_name_price_sql);
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes
```</answer>

Also, need to handle exceptions. For example, if the ALTER TABLE fails for some other reason, the function should return false.

So wrap the entire process in a try-catch block.

So the code outline:

try {

  return true;
} catch (const std::exception& e) {
  return false;
}

But also, after each ALTER TABLE, check if it was successful. Or rely on exceptions being thrown if the SQL fails.

So the code would look like:

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

    // Check and add phone_number
    if (!column_exists(conn, "phone_number")) {
      conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
    }

    // Check and add is_active
    if (!column_exists(conn, "is_active")) {
      conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Log error if needed
    return false;
  }
}

But need to implement the column_exists function.

Wait, but in the code provided, the User struct has user_id as a PrimaryKey<int>, which is auto-incrementing in the UserAfter struct. But the original table may not have auto-increment. However, the problem states that the existing User table has user_id and username. So the code is modifying the existing table, not creating a new one. So the structs are just examples, but the actual code works on the existing table.

So the code should not involve creating tables, only altering them.

Now, the helper function column_exists.

Implementing that as a lambda inside the function.

So:

auto check_column_exists = [&](const std::string& _col_name) -> bool {
  auto res = conn->execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    if (row["name"] == _col_name) {
      return true;
    }
  }
  return false;
};

Then, use this lambda to check each column.

Putting it all together.

So the code would be:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

    // Check if phone_number exists
    auto check_col = [&](const std::string& col_name) -> bool {
      auto res = conn->execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
      for (const auto& row : res) {
        if (row["name"] == col_name) {
          return true;
        }
      }
      return false;
    };

    if (!check_col("phone_number")) {
      conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
    }

    if (!check_col("is_active")) {
      conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

} // namespace schema_migration_add_column

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    conn.begin_transaction();

    }

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<A>().join<B>().on(B.a_id == A.id)
      .where(B.score > 10 && A.name.like("%x%"))
      .order_by(B.score.desc())
      .limit(5);
  return query.to_sql(sqlgen::SQLite{});
}
}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
```</answer>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {

    for (size_t i = 0; i < items.size(); ++i) {

          query_result[i].name != items[i].name ||
          query_result[i].quantity != items[i].quantity) {
        return false;
      }
    }
    return true;
  } catch (...) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <string>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyses;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    MathAnalysis analysis;
    analysis.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    analysis.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    analysis.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    analysis.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    analyses.push_back(analysis);
  }
  return analyses;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      product_id,
      name,
      ABS(price) as abs_price,
      CEIL(price) as ceil_price,
      FLOOR(price) as floor_price,
      ROUND(price, 2) as rounded_price,
      EXP(discount_rate/100) as exp_discount,
      LN(price) as ln_price,
      LOG2(quantity) as log2_quantity,
      SIN(discount_rate) as sin_discount,
      COS(discount_rate) as cos_discount,
      TAN(discount_rate) as tan_discount,
      SQRT(quantity) as sqrt_quantity
    FROM Product;
  )";

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyses;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    MathAnalysis analysis;
    analysis.product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>();
    analysis.name = row["name"].as<std::string>();
    analysis.abs_price = row["abs_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.ceil_price = row["ceil_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.floor_price = row["floor_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.rounded_price = row["rounded_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.exp_discount = row["exp_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.ln_price = row["ln_price"].as<double>();
    analysis.log2_quantity = row["log2_quantity"].as<double>();
    analysis.sin_discount = row["sin_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.cos_discount = row["cos_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.tan_discount = row["tan_discount"].as<double>();
    analysis.sqrt_quantity = row["sqrt_quantity"].as<double>();
    analyses.push_back(analysis);
  }
  return analyses;
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "trim_and_length_validation.hpp"

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = conn->select_from<UserProfile>(
      "user_id",
      "TRIM(name)",
      "LENGTH(TRIM(name))",
      "TRIM(email)",
      "LENGTH(TRIM(email))"
  )
      .where("name IS NOT NULL AND email IS NOT NULL")
      .order_by("user_id")
      .limit(100)
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<ValidatedProfile> result;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          ValidatedProfile profile;
          profile.user_id = res.get<0>();
          profile.trimmed_name = res.get<1>();
          profile.name_length = res.get<2>();
          profile.trimmed_email = res.get<3>();
          profile.email_length = res.get<4>();
          result.emplace_back(std::move(profile));
        }
        return result;
      });

  if (!query) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Query failed: " + query.error().what());
  }

  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```</answer>

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include "sqlgen.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.h"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/connect.h"

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(
    const std::vector<OrderItem>& _items) {
  // Connect to an in-memory SQLite database.
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>(':memory:');

  // Insert all OrderItem into the database.
  for (const auto& item : _items) {
    conn->insert(item);
  }

  // Prepare the SQL query.
  const auto query = R"(
SELECT 
  item_id,
  order_id,
  product_id,
  unit_price,
  quantity,
  COALESCE(discount_rate, 0) as discount_rate,
  COALESCE(tax_rate, 0) as tax_rate,
  (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
  (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100)) as discounted_total,
  (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0)/100)) as final_total,
  (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
  (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
  (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
FROM OrderItem;
)";

  // Execute the query and get the results.
  const auto rows = conn->execute(_items, query);

  // Convert the rows to OrderMetrics structs.
  std::vector<OrderMetrics> result;
  for (const auto& row : rows) {
    OrderMetrics metrics{
        .item_id = row["item_id"].as<int>(),
        .order_id = row["order_id"].as<int>(),
        .product_id = row["product_id"].as<int>(),
        .unit_price = row["unit_price"].as<double>(),
        .quantity = row["quantity"].as<int>(),
        .discount_rate = row["discount_rate"].as<double>(),
        .tax_rate = row["tax_rate"].as<double>(),
        .gross_total = row["gross_total"].as<double>(),
        .discounted_total = row["discounted_total"].as<double>(),
        .final_total = row["final_total"].as<double>(),
        .unit_cost = row["unit_cost"].as<double>(),
        .quantity_group = row["quantity_group"].as<int>(),
        .estimated_profit = row["estimated_profit"].as<double>()};
    result.push_back(metrics);
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

#endif

#include <rfl.hpp>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"

#include "sqlgen/write.hpp"

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = sqlgen::make_session(conn);
  const auto query =
      session->prepare_statement("SELECT * FROM Customer WHERE customer_id = ?;");
  query.bind_int(1, id);
  return session->template read_one<Customer>(query);
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLiteConnector>("test_db.sqlite");

  // Update the content by replacing 'foo' with 'bar'
  conn->update<Note>()
      .set(Note::content, sqlgen::expr("REPLACE(" + Note::content.str() + ", 'foo', 'bar')"))
      .execute();

  // Retrieve all updated notes
  auto notes = conn->select_from<Note>().execute();

  // Convert to UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    UpdatedNote updated_note;
    updated_note.note_id = note.note_id;
    updated_note.updated_content = note.content;
    result.push_back(updated_note);
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

namespace cascade_delete_demo {

struct Parent {

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = conn->prepare(
      "SELECT COALESCE(s.name, '') AS supplier_name, p.name AS product_name "
      "FROM Supplier s "
      "FULL OUTER JOIN Product p ON s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id"
  );

  const auto res = query.execute();

  std::vector<SupProd> result;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    SupProd sp;
    sp.supplier_name = row["supplier_name"].value();
    sp.product_name = row["product_name"].transform([](auto&& str) { return std::move(str); });
    result.emplace_back(std::move(sp));
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace concat_function {

struct Person {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string first_name;
  std::string last_name;
};

struct FullName {
  std::string full_name;
};

inline auto concat_function() {
  return sqlgen::select_from<Person>().select(
      sqlgen::make_tuple(
          sqlgen::CONCAT(sqlgen::col("first_name"), sqlgen::str(" "), sqlgen::col("last_name"))
      )
  ).to_sql();
}

}  // namespace concat_function
```

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace aggregation_arithmetic {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  double price;
};

std::string aggregation_arithmetic() {
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>(
        sqlgen::fields{sqlgen::avg(Product::price) + 10.0}
    );

    sqlite::Connection conn(":memory:");
    return conn.to_sql(query);
}

}  // namespace aggregation_arithmetic
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_index_basic {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string title;
};

inline auto create_index_basic() {
    return sqlgen::create_index<sqlgen::TableWrapper<Book>, "idx_book_title", Book::title>();
}

}  // namespace create_index_basic
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;

    auto query = update<Product>().set("stock", "stock + :qty").where("product_id = :pid").params({":qty" : qty, ":pid" : pid});
    auto exec_res = execute(txn, query);
    if (!exec_res) {
      return false;
    }
  }
  
  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.execute(
        "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
        "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) as total "
        "FROM Order o "
        "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;"
    );
    auto query = conn.select_from<OrderSummary>();
    return query.execute();
}
}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session::make(std::move(conn));

    const auto query = session->select_from<Sales>(
        sqlgen::as(sqlgen::strftime("%w", Sales::sale_time), "weekday"),
        sqlgen::sum(Sales::quantity).as("total_quantity")
    )
    .where(nullptr)
    .group_by("weekday");

    std::vector<WeekdayStats> result;
    for (const auto& row : query) {
      WeekdayStats stats;
      stats.weekday = row.get<int>("weekday");
      stats.total_quantity = row.get<int>("total_quantity");
      result.push_back(stats);
    }

    return result;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions as needed
    throw;
  }
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
